steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
Using the two given points $(0, 2)$ and $(1, -3)$:
$$m = \frac{-3 - 2}{1 - 0} = \frac{-5}{1} = -5$$

steps.title: Identify the y-intercept
steps.content:
The point $(0, 2)$ has $x = 0$, so the y-intercept is $b = 2$. The line in slope-intercept form is:
$$y = -5x + 2$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $y = 5x + 2$: slope $+5$, wrong sign — eliminate.
  • $y = -5x + 2$: slope $-5$, intercept $2$ — matches.
  • $y = x + 2$: slope $1$, wrong — eliminate.
  • $y = -x + 2$: slope $-1$, wrong — eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
